topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red 'D-' glared back at me from the printed essay. It felt like a physical blow, sinking right into my chest. I stared at the grade, my breath catching in a shallow, uneven rhythm. I remembered the late nights, the promises I made to myself about pulling an all-nighter for this assignment, the sheer confidence I’d carried into the exam hall. Now, all that effort seemed to have dissolved into a meaningless smudge on the page. My hands clenched into tight fists on the desk, the knuckles turning white. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips, a sound heavy with disappointment. I traced the ink of the poor score with a trembling finger, the cheap paper feeling flimsy beneath my touch.

[story 2]

I walked into the lecture hall feeling strangely hollowed out. The professor’s announcement echoed in my ears, and when I saw my name listed next to that stark, low mark, the air seemed to thin out. My stomach twisted into a tight knot, a cold sensation spreading outward. I avoided looking at my classmates, focusing instead on the scuff marks on the floor. "I just didn't study enough," I muttered under my breath, the words sounding thin and inadequate even to my own ears. I picked up my notebook, the cover suddenly feeling heavy and burdensome. I wanted to crumple the paper, to erase the evidence of my failure, but my fingers remained stubbornly still, hovering over the rough surface as if afraid to disturb the mess.

[story 3]

The notification popped up on my screen, a stark, undeniable verdict. A B- minus. It wasn't the catastrophe I had envisioned, but it was a significant fall from the standard I’d set for myself. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. My gaze drifted to the window, where the afternoon sun cast long, accusing shadows across the room. I picked up my pen, the weight of it suddenly immense. I started to write, but the words refused to flow. Every sentence felt clumsy, every argument weak. I reread the initial thesis statement, a wave of self-reproach washing over me, leaving a bitter taste in my mouth. I just wanted to disappear into the quiet hum of the library, wishing the entire afternoon could rewind itself.
